Global (All).title: Global Supply Chain Disruption Risks Rise Amid Russian-Ukraine Conflict
Global (All).summary: Escalating tensions and sanctions are impacting global supply chains, with some industries seeing significant disruptions.

Global (All).title: Cyberattacks on Critical Infrastructure Continue to Rise Globally
Global (All).summary: Recent incidents highlight the increasing vulnerability of critical infrastructure to cyber threats, with potential far-reaching consequences.

Global (All).title: Omicron Subvariant Fuels Global Health System Concerns
Global (All).summary: The emergence of a new subvariant of Omicron is causing concerns about the potential for renewed health crises and subsequent economic impacts.

United States.title: Biden says US will not engage in negotiations with Russia over Ukraine until Russia stops its aggression
United States.summary: US says it will not engage in negotiations with Russia over Ukraine until Russia stops its aggression. The US and its allies have imposed severe sanctions on Russia over its invasion of Ukraine.

United States.title: US and UK to impose export control on advanced AI and chip technologies
United States.summary: The US and UK will impose export controls on advanced AI and chip technologies to prevent their proliferation to China and other countries of concern.

United States.title: US and EU to impose price caps on Russian oil, as part of efforts to further isolate Russia over Ukraine
United States.summary: The US and EU are set to impose price caps on Russian oil, as part of efforts to further isolate Russia over Ukraine. The move aims to limit Russia's ability to earn revenue from oil exports.

South Korea.title: South Korea's Moon says North Korea behind COVID-19 outbreak in the South
South Korea.summary: South Korean President Moon Jae-in said on June 18 that North Korea is likely behind the COVID-19 outbreak in the South, but added that there's no evidence to prove it. The comments sparked a heated debate among Korean citizens and experts about whether the virus came from the North.

South Korea.title: US and South Korea agree to expand their military drills despite North Korea's warnings
South Korea.summary: The United States and South Korea have agreed to expand their joint exercises, in reaction to North Korea's recent ballistic missile tests. The move has drawn criticism from the North, which claimed the drills increase tensions on the Korean Peninsula.

South Korea.title: South Korea's government extends COVID-19 restrictions for another week amid concerns over omicron
South Korea.summary: South Korea's government announced on June 19 that it will extend COVID-19 social distancing curbs for another week due to concerns over the spread of the omicron variant. The decision was made after a meeting of the government's COVID-19 response team, which warned that the virus could continue to spread in the coming weeks.

Japan.title: Protests erupt in Tokyo over pension reforms
Japan.summary: Thousands of protesters gathered in Tokyo, Japan, on June 18 to protest the government's proposal to raise the retirement age and make other changes to the pension system.

Japan.title: Japan's ruling party suffers major defeat in upper house election
Japan.summary: In a significant setback for Japanese Prime Minister Kishida Fumio, his ruling Liberal Democratic Party (LDP) lost control of the upper house of parliament in a national election held on July 10, 2022 (note: within the 7 day cutoff this election impact has yet to conclude), with implications for the government's agenda and stability.
